drive.title: Google Drive
drive.no_credentials_body: Without this file, extraction runs in local mode — data will not be synced to Google Drive.
drive.how_to: Learn how to get the file →
drive.or_local: Or start without Drive to save locally only
drive.aria_open_browser: Authenticate Google Drive — opens browser for login
channel.title: YouTube Channel
channel.error_invalid: Invalid URL. Use the format youtube.com/@Channel
ops.title: Operations
banner.local_desc: without Drive authentication, data will be saved only in the cerebro/youtube/ folder.
progress.title: Progress
guide.title: How to use BrainIAc?
guide.subtitle: Step-by-step guide
guide.step1: In the Extraction tab, paste any YouTube channel URL and click Confirm Channel.
guide.step2: Click Start Extraction and select content types. The engine downloads captions automatically.
guide.step3: In the Repository tab, view extracted files. Also add PDFs, documents and text to the knowledge base.
guide.step4: In the Configure Agent tab, click Index Now. Local Ollama processes everything — no API key needed.
log.title: Real-Time Log
log.complete_steps: Complete the steps on the side to begin.
agent.embed_key_note: Claude has no embeddings API. Provide a Gemini key for vectorization.
agent.get_key_gemini: Get Gemini key at Google AI Studio
agent.index_note_new: Index the extracted files to enable chat with the agent.
agent.index_note_update: Reindex to update the base with newly extracted videos.
agent.chat_empty_no_index: Index the knowledge base above to start chatting with the agent.
ops.types_modal_title: What would you like to extract?
ops.types_modal_subtitle: Select the content types from the channel.
onboarding.s1_body: A pipeline that turns any YouTube channel into an AI-queryable knowledge base. Here's how it works in 4 steps.
onboarding.s2_body: Paste any YouTube channel URL (e.g. youtube.com/@ChannelName). This defines the scope of extraction and the AI agent.
onboarding.s3_body: Toggle Drive to sync extracted files to the cloud. Without it, everything is saved locally in the cerebro/youtube/ folder.
onboarding.s4_body: Choose which content types to extract (videos, shorts, lives…) and click Start Extraction. The engine maps and downloads captions automatically.
onboarding.s5_body: In the AI Agent tab, set your API key (Gemini, OpenAI, or Claude), index the knowledge base, and ask questions about any channel content.
modal.subtitle: What would you like to do with the extracted data?
modal.drive_desc_connected: Files synced to Drive. Open NotebookLM and import the folder to use them as context.
modal.drive_desc_local: Files were saved locally in cerebro/youtube/. Authenticate Google Drive to sync them.
modal.agent_desc: Set your API key, index the knowledge base, and ask questions directly about channel content.
modal.agent_configured: Agent configured and ready to use
modal.agent_not_configured: Configure your API key in the AI Agent tab
agent.index_what: Indexing reads the extracted .txt files, splits the content into chunks, and generates vector embeddings stored locally. This enables the agent to perform semantic search over channel content.
agent.index_prereq_key: Configure an API key above to enable indexing.
agent.index_prereq_channel: Configure a channel in the sidebar to enable indexing.
guide.step5: Use the chat button (bottom right corner) to ask questions about the channel content.
guide.step6: In the Report tab, track coverage: extracted videos, failures and statistics per channel.
